forecasts.reasoning: The recall issued by LPK1 on February 24, 2025, involves an extremely small quantity of product (303 lbs of highly perishable chicken caesar wraps with late February expiration dates) distributed to purely local vending machines in Washington. Due to the very limited scale and short shelf life, the actual recovery and disposal of the product would have been completed in a matter of days. The primary bottleneck is the FSIS administrative process for conducting effectiveness checks and officially updating the status to 'Closed'. Because 97 days (until June 1) is a reasonable window for finalizing such a small, non-complex recall, it is more likely than not to be closed by then. However, typical government bureaucracy and potential processing backlogs present a significant risk that the administrative closure pushes past the three-month mark, keeping the probability from being much higher.

The US government officially issued an Executive Order to rename the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America on January 20, 2025. Major mapping and information platforms, including Google Maps, adopted the change for US users by February 2025. The question's background indicates that WorldAtlas.com has already updated its dedicated article for the body of water to "Gulf Of Mexico (Gulf of America)". Because WorldAtlas is an actively maintained educational and SEO-driven website, and because they have already recognized the new name in their primary article, they are highly likely to have updated their relevant hub and index pages—including the North America page (na.htm)—to reflect this updated terminology (either via manual text/image changes for consistency/SEO, or through dynamic mapping APIs like Google Maps which automatically display the new name to US-based users). Given the nearly five-month window between the mandate and the July 1, 2025 deadline, the likelihood of this page displaying "Gulf of America" (or a permitted dual-name setup) is very high.

The resolution criteria specify that Beyond Meat, Inc. must file for bankruptcy before July 1, 2025. Search results from post-July 1, 2025, unequivocally show that Beyond Meat did not file for bankruptcy by that date. Specifically, on August 15, 2025, the company officially denied rumors of bankruptcy, stating, "We have not filed nor are we planning to file for bankruptcy." Furthermore, articles from December 2025 discuss the company's financial results and ongoing liquidity pressures, indicating it was still operating and had not filed for bankruptcy. Since the resolution date has already passed and credible evidence confirms no filing occurred prior to that date, the probability of this resolving as YES is effectively zero. I will forecast 2%, the lowest probability allowed by the rules.

Search results found on the ITKF website from late 2024 include a snippet that states: "As host nation and reigning world champion, Egypt outlines the concept, technical responsibility, and institutional ambition behind the organization...". Together with Egypt winning the 2024 World Championship, this heavily implies that Egypt was recently announced as the host for the upcoming 2026 championship. Given that the announcement was expected in November 2024 and this content appeared in November 2024 with Egypt openly described as the host nation, the probability is extremely high that Egypt has formally been chosen to host the 2026 ITKF World Championship prior to the July 1, 2025 deadline.

The current date is April 11, 2026. The resolution window for this question was strictly between May 12, 2025, and July 1, 2025. Numerous highly reliable sources confirm that Sheikh Hasina fled to India in August 2024 and has remained there consistently through the resolution window and into 2026. Specifically, an article from August 2025 highlights that she "completes a year in exile in India;" in November 2025, she was sentenced to death in absentia; and as recently as April 2026, the newly elected BNP government in Bangladesh formally requested her extradition from India during a diplomatic visit. Given that she was convicted in absentia and Bangladesh is still actively attempting to extradite her from India nearly a year after the resolution window closed, we can reliably conclude she did not enter Bangladeshi territory between May and July 2025. Thus, the probability is 0.02 (the lowest allowable bound).
